### Post by gohanssjn on 2007-05-16
I installed the Ubuntu repo. NTFS read/write thing, and then uninstalled it.  Now I cannot see my two NTFS drives.

Fstab:
> # /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=31221ac4-b30b-4895-95a0-faf0469a347c / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=E0FC7BECFC7BBAFC /media/sda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdb1 :
UUID=2A744D7B744D4AB1 /media/sdb1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=2cd21341-7d0f-46f3-ab17-26fa8c5ff993 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0

Fdisk:

> Disk /dev/sda: 120.0 GB, 120034123776 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 14593 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sda1               1       11982    96244391    7  HPFS/NTFS
/dev/sda2   *       11983       14347    18996862+  83  Linux
/dev/sda3           14348       14593     1975995   82  Linux swap / Solaris

Disk /dev/sdb: 163.9 GB, 163928604672 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 19929 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sdb1               1       19929   160079661    7  HPFS/NTFS

Disk /dev/sdc: 2055 MB, 2055021056 bytes
16 heads, 63 sectors/track, 3981 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 1008 * 512 = 516096 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sdc1   *           1        3982     2006825    b  W95 FAT32


Anyone see anything wrong?

---

### Post by gohanssjn on 2007-05-16
Solved:  Somehow I uninstalled ntfs3g0 >.>

---

### Post by logos34 on 2007-05-16
> # Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=E0FC7BECFC7BBAFC /media/sda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdb1 :
UUID=2A744D7B744D4AB1 /media/sdb1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1

If you made a backup of your fstab file before you installed the ntfs-3g driver, just copy it back.

Or change the above entries to this:
> 
/dev/sda1 /media/sda1**ntfs defaults,nls=utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 1**
/dev/sdb1 /media/sdb1**ntfs defaults,nls=utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 1**

---

### Post by logos34 on 2007-05-16

So, do your ntfs partitions automount at startup?
